item.title: Sanofi Tolebrutinib (Cenrifki) EU Approval
item.description:
• Summary: The European Medicines Agency's CHMP has recommended approval of Sanofi's tolebrutinib (brand name Cenrifki) for certain patients with multiple sclerosis. This follows a rejection from the FDA earlier this year. The CHMP backing marks a significant win for Sanofi in the competitive MS space. Tolebrutinib is a next-generation BTK inhibitor designed to cross the blood-brain barrier and target both peripheral and central nervous system components of MS.
  • MoAs: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News, STAT News

item.title: Foundayo Psychedelics Prescriptions
item.description:
• Summary: The company Foundayo is enabling prescriptions for psychedelic therapies, likely for mental health conditions such as depression and PTSD. The article discusses the broader landscape, including Regeneron, Amgen, Novo Nordisk, and BridgeBio, as well as the Trump administration's policies. Psychedelic-assisted therapy is gaining traction as a treatment for neurological and psychiatric disorders, and Foundayo aims to facilitate access through its prescription platform.
  • MoAs: Psychedelics (e.g., psilocybin): primarily act as agonists at the serotonin 5-HT2A receptor, promoting neuroplasticity, emotional processing, and alleviation of symptoms in mood and anxiety disorders.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive
